multiplechoiceresponse.label: Looking at the SHAP summary plot shown above, which of the following cannot be determined from this plot alone?

choicegroup.choice: That features like cp_0 and ca_0 have larger overall impact on the model than features such as restecg_1
That higher values of oldpeak tend to push predictions toward lower risk in the model.
Why a specific patient received a predicted probability of approximately 0.68
That the effect of age varies across patients rather than having a single fixed direction.

div.p: The SHAP summary plot displays aggregated SHAP values across all patients, showing which features matter most overall, how feature values relate to prediction direction, and whether effects vary across individuals. However, it does not explain any specific patient’s prediction. Explaining why one patient received a particular probability requires a local SHAP explanation that isolates that individual’s feature contributions.
